content.problemStatement:
You have to place (N+1)(N+1)(N+1) points in an NNN-dimensional space so that:


All these (N+1)(N+1)(N+1) points are 111 unit distance away from the origin (0,0,…,0)(0, 0, \dots, 0)(0,0,…,0).
The minimum distance between all pairs of these (N+1)(N+1)(N+1) points is maximized.
If there are many solutions, output any of them. Your solution will be accepted if the absolute error of the minimum distance between all pairs of these (N+1)(N+1)(N+1) points is within 10−610^{-6}10−6 of the judge’s solution.


Input Format
At first, you will be given TTT, the number of test cases. Then TTT lines will follow. In each line, there will be a single integer NNN.



Output Format
For each input, output (N+1)(N+1)(N+1) lines. Each line should have NNN floating point numbers, representing all the NNN coordinates for one NNN-dimensional point. Output a blank line between cases.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then T lines follow, each containing a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output (N+1)(N+1)(N+1) lines, each containing N floating point numbers representing the coordinates of a point in N-dimensional space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case where N=1, the points are placed at 1.00 and -1.00 on the 1-dimensional line. Both points are 1 unit away from the origin, and the minimum distance between them is 2.00, which is maximized. For the second test case where N=2, the points are placed at specific coordinates on the 2-dimensional plane. Each point is 1 unit away from the origin, and the minimum distance between any pair of points is maximized.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.expectedOutput: 256^3 points with coordinates in the range [-1, 1]

hidden.label: edge case
hidden.expectedOutput: 8 points with coordinates in the range [-1, 1]

solution.approach: To maximize the minimum distance between points in N-dimensional space, we can use the concept of a regular simplex. A regular simplex is a polytope with a high degree of symmetry. In this case, we can place the points at the vertices of a regular simplex centered at the origin. The distance between any two vertices of a regular simplex is the same, which maximizes the minimum distance between points.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * (N+1)^3) — because we need to generate (N+1)^3 points for each test case
solution.spaceComplexity: O((N+1)^3) — because we need to store the coordinates of all points
